title: myrica pubescens var. interrupta
intro: Myrica pubescens var. interrupta, also known as the interrupted bayberry, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the Myricaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of compounds like myricetin and betulinic acid. The health benefits of interrupta include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of interrupta can be used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and uses.
